title: Magnet Properties Matching Tool
instructions_for_student: Tap an activity on the left, then tap the magnetic property on the right that explains it. Correct matches draw a coloured line between them and turn green. A wrong tap shakes the property card and shows a helpful hint on the activity. Match all four to see the summary!

activities.text: Ring magnets floating on a stand
Steel balls guided through a maze
Paper clip rising in water without magnet touching it
Toy cars pushing apart on a track

properties.label: Like poles repel
properties.description: Two poles of the same type push each other away
properties.hint: The ring magnets sit on a rod with the same poles facing — they push apart and the upper one floats!

properties.label: Magnetic effect through cardboard
properties.description: Magnetic force passes through non-magnetic materials
properties.hint: The magnet moves BELOW the cardboard tray. Its force passes right through to guide the steel balls above.

properties.label: Magnetic effect through glass & water
properties.description: Magnetic force passes through glass and water
properties.hint: Glass and water are non-magnetic — the magnet's force passes straight through to attract the steel clip inside.

properties.label: Like poles repel
properties.description: Matching poles on adjacent objects cause repulsion
properties.hint: Each car has the same pole at the front — they repel each other and roll apart without touching!

agent_readme.architecture: click-to-select + click-to-match + animated SVG bezier connector lines (mirrors MetalsNonmetalsUseMatcher reference pattern)

interaction_flow.step1: Student taps an activity card (left column) — card highlights with a coloured glow border and scales up slightly
interaction_flow.step2: Status bar at the bottom changes to 'Now tap the matching property on the right →'
interaction_flow.step3_correct: A curved SVG bezier line animates from the activity to the property. Both cards turn green. Score increments. Matched-property mini tag appears on the activity card.
interaction_flow.step3_wrong: Property card shakes. A contextual hint panel slides in beneath the activity card. No connection is drawn. Student can try again.
interaction_flow.completion: After all 4 correct matches, a gradient summary overlay slides up with confetti, a per-match breakdown, and a Play Again button.

palette.primary: #533086  (deep purple — header gradient start, activity labels)
palette.accent1: #FF7212  (orange — third activity, progress bar end, status bar arrow)

design_system.svg_lines: Cubic bezier M 0 y1 C 34 y1, 34 y2, 68 y2 — 68px wide centre column, recalculated via getBoundingClientRect on every match + window resize

activities.description: Left-column items the student taps first

item_fields.color: string — hex; used for border, label badge, SVG line, and glow shadow
item_fields.propertyId: string — must match one property id
item_fields.icon: string — emoji shown on the card

properties.description: Right-column drop targets — shuffled on each load

item_fields.label: string — short property name shown on the card
item_fields.description: string — one-line explanation shown beneath label (hide with showDescription: false)
item_fields.hint: string — shown on the activity card after a wrong attempt (hide with showHints: false)
item_fields.chapterRef: string — section badge shown on property card (hide with showChapterRef: false)

flags.showHints: boolean (default true)  — show hint panel on wrong attempt
flags.showChapterRef: boolean (default true)  — show chapter section badge on property cards
flags.showDescription: boolean (default true)  — show description line beneath property label

responsive_notes.font_sizes: clamp(11px, 1.8vw, 12px) for card text; clamp(13px, 2.5vw, 16px) for header
responsive_notes.svg_canvas: height 100% — stretches with content; overflow: visible so lines are never clipped
responsive_notes.layout: Three-column flex row: flex:1 | 68px fixed | flex:1; wraps gracefully on narrow viewports

customisation_examples.note: Replace activities and properties arrays entirely. Any number of pairs is supported.

customisation_examples.config: { showHints: false, showChapterRef: false, showDescription: false }

customisation_examples.note: Extend both arrays. LINE_COLORS array inside the component cycles through 5 colours — add more if needed.
